verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted serine protease of the kallikrein family. It carries a signal peptide and is constitutively secreted into the extracellular milieu (seminal plasma, prostatic fluid, serum) with no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or documented stable covalent tethering to a cell-surface partner. Its detection in serum/plasma as a soluble protease and its role in cleaving pro-PSA extracellularly confirm it is a secreted-only protein. No wash-resistant cell-surface pool, TM precursor form, or cell-state-induced outer-leaflet display has been documented.
